channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Kenyan villagers sue BP over pollution from 1980s oil exploration
item.description: Villagers in Kenya are suing British Petroleum, saying oil exploration by Amoco in the 1980s left toxic pollution.

item.title: Palestinian boy mourns father killed in Israeli strike on security post
item.description: A Palestinian boy mourns his father, one of three people killed in an Israeli strike on a security post in Gaza.

item.title: US issues new Cuba sanctions as UN experts warn of ‘energy starvation’
item.description: Latest sanctions target military-controlled conglomerate, come as effective US fuel blockade continues.

item.title: ‘This is my last voice note’: how piracy returned to Somalia and Yemen
item.description: Three ships were hijacked near the Gulf of Aden, raising alarms over key sea routes. Is piracy returning to Somalia?

item.title: Gulf states urge UN action to ensure Strait of Hormuz safety
item.description: Draft resolution demands Iran halt attacks, pin-point mines and ensure passage of humanitarian aid through strait.

item.title: Can China help end the Iran war?
item.description: Chinese and Iranian foreign ministers meet while a tense ceasefire holds.

item.title: Inside the Israeli bloc that could topple Netanyahu
item.description: Netanyahu’s rivals unite ahead of elections, but is this alliance strong enough to actually unseat him?

item.title: ISIL-linked Australian women arrested at airports
item.description: Three ISIL-linked Australian women have been arrested at airports in Melbourne and Sydney.

item.title: Israel’s attacks and pressure sowing seeds for division in tense Lebanon
item.description: Analysts say Israel aiming to exploit Lebanon’s divisions to force concessions amid military and political pressures.

item.title: Brazil’s Lula meets Trump amid efforts to avert new US trade tariffs
item.description: Brazilian officials are hoping for the best outcome from the meeting, given tensions over Trump's tariff policy.

item.title: Man pleads guilty to Colorado firebombing, groups call for family reprieve
item.description: June 2025 attack targeted supporters of Israeli captives in Gaza; Advocates say family of assailant unjustly targeted.

item.title: US sanctions Iraqi minister accused of aiding Iran oil sales
item.description: US sanctions target Iraq's deputy oil minister for allegedly aiding Iran in bypassing restrictions on oil exports.

item.title: Protests in Bolivia escalate amid economic turmoil and policy demands
item.description: Clashes erupt in La Paz as workers protest against rising fuel prices and damaged vehicles caused by subsidy cuts.

item.title: EU closely monitoring hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
item.description: The EU says it is closely monitoring suspected hantavirus cases aboard the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius

item.title: Superbike rider killed in crash during qualifying in Northern Ireland
item.description: Rider is 20th fatality at North West 200 but first since 2016, race due to celebrate its centenary in 2029.

item.title: Why has the US sanctioned Iraq’s deputy oil minister for helping Iran?
item.description: The US accuses Ali Maarij al-Bahadly of helping Iran avoid sanctions and export its oil through Iraq.

item.title: Trump calls for ‘incitement’ charges against top Democrat Hakeem Jeffries
item.description: US president says House minority leader should be indicted over 'maximum warfare' remarks in relation to election maps.

item.title: WHO confirms five cases of hantavirus linked to cruise ship
item.description: Hantaviruses are a group of viruses carried by rodents that can cause severe disease in humans.

item.title: US pushing Israeli de-escalation ahead of new talks: Lebanese official
item.description: Talks between Lebanese and Israeli delegations to be held in Washington, DC, next week, the official tells Al Jazeera.

item.title: Lebanon Latest Israeli strikes Beirut despite ‘ceasefire’
item.description: Israel has carried out its first airstrike on Beirut’s southern suburbs since the April 16 US-brokered ceasefire.

item.title: Iran’s president says he has spoken to the country’s Supreme Leader
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian says he has met with Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: The BJP’s Bengal victory exposes the erosion of Indian democracy
item.description: The BJP's victory after sweeping electoral roll revision raises profound questions over the integrity of the vote.

item.title: Iran’s President Pezeshkian seeks to quash divided leadership narrative
item.description: Tehran emphasises solidarity among top officials as Washington pressures Iran during Strait of Hormuz disputes.

item.title: US judge releases purported Jeffrey Epstein suicide note
item.description: Judge does not make claim on authenticity of document, filed in court as part of criminal case of former cell mate.

item.title: What’s ‘Mbappe Out’, and why are fans demanding Real Madrid star’s exit?
item.description: An online petition calling for Mbappe's ouster has gone viral and collected 33 million signatures.
